expectations.text: Permission defined in restricted_reports.permissions.yml with 'title' key (permission string must match what route uses)
expectations.evidence: restricted_reports.permissions.yml line 1-2 defines 'view restricted reports' with title: 'View restricted reports'. The route in restricted_reports.routing.yml line 7 uses _permission: 'view restricted reports' -- strings match exactly.

expectations.text: Route _permission requirement references the EXACT permission string defined in .permissions.yml
expectations.evidence: restricted_reports.routing.yml line 7: _permission: 'view restricted reports'. restricted_reports.permissions.yml line 1: 'view restricted reports:' -- exact match confirmed.

expectations.text: If custom access checking code exists, it returns AccessResult objects (AccessResult::allowed(), AccessResult::forbidden(), AccessResult::neutral()) -- NOT bare booleans (TRUE/FALSE)
expectations.evidence: No custom access checking code exists. The module uses only the declarative _permission route requirement in routing.yml. No AccessResult, no bare boolean returns found in any module files. The expectation is vacuously satisfied (no custom access code to violate it).

expectations.text: If AccessResult objects are used, they include ->addCacheContexts(['user.permissions']) or ->addCacheTags() for cache-aware access
expectations.evidence: No AccessResult objects are used anywhere in the module. The module relies entirely on the declarative _permission route requirement, so this conditional expectation is vacuously satisfied.

expectations.text: Does NOT use hook_permission() (D7 pattern) -- permissions are in .permissions.yml
expectations.evidence: Grep for 'hook_permission' across all module files returned no matches. Permissions are correctly defined in restricted_reports.permissions.yml.

expectations.text: Does NOT implement manual CSRF token checking -- would use _csrf_token route option if CSRF protection needed
expectations.evidence: Grep for 'csrf' and 'token' (case-insensitive) across all module files returned no matches. No manual CSRF token checking is implemented.

expectations.text: The module enables successfully: ddev drush en restricted_reports returns exit code 0
expectations.evidence: Command 'ddev drush en restricted_reports -y' returned '[notice] Already installed: restricted_reports' with EXIT_CODE=0.

expectations.text: E2E: Anonymous gets 403 -- navigate to /reports/restricted as anonymous and verify access denied
E2E: Admin gets 200 -- navigate to /reports/restricted as admin and verify page loads
expectations.evidence: After authenticating as admin (uid=1) via drush uli one-time login, curl -sk -b cookies -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://d10-access-with.ddev.site/reports/restricted returned HTTP 200.
